11-Oxygenated C19 Steroids Are the Predominant Androgens in Polycystic Ovary Syndrome

evidence_entities.text: serum concentrations of the classic androgens testosterone (P < 0.001), androstenedione (P < 0.001), and dehydroepiandrosterone (P < 0.01) were significantly increased in PCOS
serum 11-oxygenated androgens 11β-hydroxyandrostenedione, 11-ketoandrostenedione, 11β-hydroxytestosterone, and 11-ketotestosterone were significantly higher in PCOS than in control subjects
The proportionate contribution of 11-oxygenated to total serum androgens was significantly higher in patients with PCOS compared with control subjects [53.0% vs 44.0%; P < 0.0001]
Serum 11β-hydroxyandrostenedione and 11-ketoandrostenedione correlated significantly with markers of insulin resistance
11-oxygenated androgens constitute the majority of the circulating, unconjugated androgen excess in PCOS, which strongly suggests that they are important contributors to PCOS-related hyperandrogenism
the first step of the 11-oxygenated androgen pathway is dependent on the 11β-hydroxylation of A4 to 11OHA4 by adrenal cytochrome P450 11β-hydroxylase
